Server._comment: TCP port the bridge listens on. Override with `--port N` on the command line; CLI wins over this value.

Tracing._comment: Per-request audit JSON capture. OFF by default — turn on to debug a specific issue, then turn off (the JSON files contain full request and response bodies, including user prompts). Each request emits 4 files (inbound-req, inbound-resp, upstream-req, upstream-resp) sharing the same seq. Directory is relative to the .exe unless absolute.

Routing._comment: nginx-style locations. Each location is a self-contained closure: 'When' matches (leaves: Model/Effort/Header; composites: AllOf/AnyOf; top-level fields = implicit AllOf), 'Use' fully describes what to do (Model swap, EffortMap remapping, Headers Set/Remove). First-match-wins; no chain, no fall-through. Header Set/Remove restricted to a whitelist (anthropic-beta, Editor-Version, Editor-Plugin-Version, Copilot-Integration-Id, X-GitHub-Api-Version). Use 'anthropic-beta:pattern' in Remove for token-level strip with trailing '*' wildcard.

Locations.Note: opus-4.7 + 1M beta → 1m-internal (4.7 base only goes to 200k ctx; the -1m-internal sibling unlocks 1M). max→xhigh because 1m-internal is the only id that natively accepts xhigh. opus-4.8 deliberately NOT in this rule — Copilot's opus-4.8 natively supports 1M context without any beta or model swap (probed 2026-06-05: 260k-token prompts return 200 with and without context-1m-2025-08-07), so 4.8 + 1M beta falls through to identity passthrough.
opus-4.6 + 1M beta → 4.6-1m variant (4.6-1m tops out at high, so no max→xhigh EffortMap)

OutboundBeta._comment: anthropic-beta tokens stripped from EVERY outbound request, regardless of model. Patterns use the same trailing-* wildcard as Headers.Remove. Operators on a different Copilot backend can edit this list — e.g. AWS Bedrock-only tenants who want structured_outputs back can delete the structured-outputs-* entry.

ResponseModelRewrite._comment: When true, rewrites `message.model` in the response (both buffered and the message_start SSE event) back to the model id the client originally requested — so Claude Code's session log and ccusage report claude-opus-4-8 instead of the back-end variant the bridge routed to (e.g. claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal). Turn off to let downstream tooling see the real back-end model.

UpstreamRetry._comment: Idempotent retry of transient upstream connection failures (net_http_client_execution_error / net_http_ssl_connection_failed) on POST /v1/messages. Only retries when the failure occurs BEFORE response headers arrive (request body never processed upstream, so re-sending is safe) — never mid-stream. MaxRetries=0 disables. Backoff: BaseDelayMs * BackoffMultiplier^(attempt-1), clamped to MaxDelayMs.
